pattern: Removing a clashing adjacent pair can expose a brand-new clash right where the gap closes, which is exactly the situation a stack handles for free: push characters, and pop whenever the top clashes with the next one.
hints: Walk the string once, keeping a stack of characters you've committed to so far.
Before pushing the next character, compare it to the character on top of the stack -- if they're the same letter but opposite case, they clash, so pop the stack instead of pushing.
Because popping can bring a new pair to the top that also clashes (the gap just closed), the stack approach resolves that automatically -- no need to rescan from the start.

edge_inputs.description: one clashing pair in the middle
same letter same case is not a clash
removing an inner pair reveals a new clash between the outer letters
clash appears only after an earlier removal cascades all the way through

stress.description: random mixed-case strings from a tiny alphabet to force lots of clashes and cascades
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    letters = 'aAbBcC'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
